summary.narrative: Across his term, the strongest documented delivery is fiscal: Miami reported $200M+ in reserves under the FY2024-2025 budget after recovering from a deficit, consistent with the campaign-era reserves pledge. On climate and homelessness, Suarez initiated formal actions (a carbon-neutral-by-2050 resolution and a 'functional zero' homelessness plan announcement) but the record lacks Tier-1 confirmation of completed outcomes, and his homelessness figures were publicly contested. Police-strengthening and the personal-accessibility pledge show little or no documented follow-through in the supplied material.
summary.grade_rationale: Most tracked promises are directional, low-clarity commitments with only one showing a measurable delivered outcome (reserves); the others sit at early milestone stages with limited or contested verification and no Tier-1 delivery anchors. The pattern is partial movement on broadly worded goals rather than completed, measurable deliverables, constrained by the mayor's largely non-voting role in a commission-controlled government.

promises.text: Increase the city's emergency financial reserves.
what_happened: 2025-01-16: City reported more than $200 million in reserves per the 2024-2025 budget after recovering from a deficit (c89).

data_gaps: The vast majority of promise candidates were presidential-campaign statements (c12-c52), generic '100-day plan' boilerplate (c72-c80), or in-office/press items that fail the campaign-attribution test and were excluded. Tier-1 delivery anchors are scarce (only c72/c88 are Tier-1, and c72 is unrelated boilerplate), so no D3/D4 delivery scores rest on Tier-1 verification — reviewer should confirm. Reserves item (c89) is Tier-3 reporting of a budget figure; police and accessibility promises have essentially no verification evidence and are flagged LIMITED EVIDENCE. Several candidate dates (c10, c13, c24) postdate or predate the relevant window and were treated as non-qualifying.
